title: Real-world evaluation of primary prophylactic use of granulocyte colony-stimulating factor in patients with metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer treated with triplet therapy.
abstract: Triplet therapy with androgen deprivation therapy (ADT), docetaxel, and androgen receptor signaling inhibitors improves outcomes in metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (mCSPC), but hematologic toxicity remains a major concern. The role of primary prophylactic granulocyte colony-stimulating factor (G-CSF) in real-world practice remains unclear. We evaluated its impact on toxicity and treatment delivery.
trial_name: Darolutamide in nonmetastatic, castration-resistant prostate cancer
